publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-22 23:16:23 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, publications, newspapers, printed material notes and organizational records.
did.abstract: Collection consists of documents, correspondence and publications generated by leftist political groups active in the Ithaca, New York area from 1966 to 1972.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Lawrence Kramer was a member of the Class of 1970 at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of documents, correspondence and publications generated by leftist political groups active in the Ithaca, New York area from 1966 to 1972. Also, material concerning political events and disturbances, such as the takeover of Willard Straight Hall, Cornell University, April 1969; the Cornell draft resistance, and the involvement of Bruce Dancis and Daniel Berrigan (1966-1968); the Cornell Trojan Horse incident, January 1967; and anti-Vietnam War protests. Also, documentation of the activities and internal evolution of organizations including The Office and the Glad Day Press (1966-1971); the national and campus branch of Students for a Democratic Society (1967-1969); the Cornell Draft Resistance (1967-1968); National Caucus of Labor Committees, and its publications, Solidarity, New Solidarity, Crisis, and Campaigner; the Socialist Labor Committee (1971-1972), including its national publications, Crisis, Quantum, and Perspectives; the Socialist Labor Committee in Ithaca, New York, and its publications, The Eleventh Hour and ILR Voice; the Socialist Workers' Party; the Worker-Student Alliance and their publication, New Left Notes;; the Young Socialist Alliance; the New Left; the Ithaca Labor Committee; the Independent Socialists; the Independent Radical Coalition; the Cornell Mobilization Direct Action Project; National Mobilization to End the War in Vietnam; New American Movement; Progressive Labor Party; The Resistance, Ithaca, New York; and the Ithaca Neighborhood Center.

Also, materials on the Revolutionary Youth Movement and Revolutionary Youth Movement II, and their publications Fire and Liberated Guardian; and Students for a Constructive Foreign Policy, Cornell University. Printed material includes magazines, newsletters, pamphlets, brochures and leaflets, and internal documents such as position papers, proposals and drafts of documents. Other material includes published and unpublished commentaries and articles, as well as political journals, meeting notes, correspondence and notes of Lawrence Kramer; government files obtained through requests under the Freedom of Information Act; clippings and editorial material concerning left groups and events; lists of signers of public petitions; university disciplinary citations; buttons and other memorabilia. Some material has been annotated by Kramer and Peter Agree (Cornell University Class of 1969).
There is also information on early left-wing activities of Lyndon LaRouche, then using the alias Lyn Marcus.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Donor retains literary rights to the notes, drafts, and published materials written by him contained in the collection.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Lawrence Felix Kramer, Collector. Papers, #37-7-2151. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: The Ithaca Labor Committee, the Socialist Labor Committee, the National Caucus of Labor Committees

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note: in both SLC and NCLC files, there is sometimes a fine line between internal and public documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 periodicals including The May Student Strike and Everything You Always Wanted to Know About the New Priorities Movement

did.unittitle: NCLC, Fraser faction replies to NCLC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 67 documents + 9 documents reporting on French left.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 30 documents. (Note: Ithaca SLC internal and public, including The Eleventh Hour and the !LR Voice, are filed as part of 6-9.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 issues (I expect to locate and provide a complete set of the approx. 20 issues.)

did.unittitle: NCLC and SLC study group outlines, critiques, and bibliographies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 24 documents, and Lawrence Kramer critique of Cornell Human Development course.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 documents, and two earlier Marcus articles from 1954 and 1961.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 70 documents, and 10 issues of an internal discussion bullet.

did.unittitle: NCLC: Lyndon LaRouche (nom de plume Lyn Marcus) book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 chapters in draft plus 10-page Jitter to Lawrence Kramer replying to criticisms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 documents, including letters of resignation from former SLC members who returned to the NCLC.

did.unittitle: Students for a Democratic Society and its factions and split offs; National Caucus of Labor Committees periodicals
SDS final national convention, Chicago, internal
SDS national public documents on Cuba
SDS NYC region, CAW arts magazine
SDS, critiques of SDS by LC, Chicago Revolutionary Socialists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 issues of Fire Magazine (faction's successor to New Left Notes) 2 issues of Liberated Guardian.

did.unittitle: NCLC Solidarity, New Solidarity, Philadelphia Crisis newspapers

Cornell events and antiwar groups, Students for a Democratic Society National and Cornell Draft Resistance, Lawrence Kramer Selective Service files. Periodicals from Cornell, Ithaca, and the Glad Day Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 documents, 13 pages research notes, Lawrence Kramer paper.

National Mobilization to End the War

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 internal (including contact list) and 4 public documents

scopecontent.p: 6 documents and letter from Ray Mungo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 24 documents and 2 issues of Son of Cornell

scopecontent.p: Cornell United Religious Work Fast for Peace, 8 documents and clips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 39 documents and Cornell disciplinary actions and clips.

Lawrence Kramer correspondence to Selective Service System
Lawrence Kramer American Civil Liberties Union lawsuit

scopecontent.p: Letters from Robert Kennedy (original) and J.W. Fullbright (copy).

did.unittitle: Lawrence Kramer chronological notebooks on study groups, internal meetings
Cornell leftists' and SDSers' Cornell Daily Sun columns

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I am not yet convinced that the proctor is a Horse's Ass ... I am convinced - button controversy - 2 documents, clips and buttons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Magazine, leaflet, Lawrence Kramer draft article and clips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 documents and registration list and clips.

did.unittitle: Cornell participation in April 15 and October 21 national demonstrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 issues of Mobilizer, 9 documents and clips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newman K. Perry 12 demonstration and arrest, NY Stock Exchange, H. Rap Brown, Committee of Concerned Honkies, Abbie Hoffman in Ithaca, 27 documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 documents including disciplinary citation with lists of participants.

did.unittitle: Ithaca Neighborhood Center descriptive & PR material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Previously Ithaca Neighborhood College, 12 documents + 4 letters.

did.unittitle: Cornell administration communications about draft, SOS
Ithaca antiwar and left groups miscellaneous
Cornell Students for a Constructive Foreign Policy
Cornell SDS post-split internal and public

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 SDS responses, 14 university statements and press releases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 issues (complete) of Notes from the Green Piano, brochures from Buffalo SDS, Niagara Liberation Front, Seattle Liberation Front ( where Niagara Reg. organizers later went), documents (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manchild in the Corporate State (Cornell), Who Rules Columbia ? , How Harvard Rules, Mexico 1968 and University-Military Complexes (North American Committee on Latin America, Dependency in the !970's (Africa Research Group).

did.unittitle: The Office and the Glad Day Press internal
New Left Notes and other left publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Radical Education Press (Michigan), New England Free Press (Massachusetts), Southern Students Organizing Committee (Tennessee), others - 18 pamphlets and documents.

did.unittitle: Radical Teacher & Radicals in the Professions Newsletter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: And RIP Speech - 1 issue and 3 issues.

did.unittitle: Workers World, Youth Against War & Fascism
Series II. Lawrence Kramer Freedom of Information Act Files and Buttons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Duplicates & material to be copied and returned to files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Cornell SDS, national SDS, NCLC, Ithaca LC, SLC, approx. 200 documents and publications.

did.unittitle: Lawrence Kramer Freedom of Information Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 letters to government; correspondence from Freedom of Information Act officers, Clarence Kelly and other high officials: 9 from Justice Dept., 3 from State Dept., 8 from FBI, 10 from CIA (check #'s).

did.unittitle: Documents released after appeals and reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents from Bruce Dancis FOIA requests. 13 pages; 29 pages; 24 pages and Dancis letter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 documents to return to Box 3, folder 29; 12 documents to return to Box 3 folder 13; other miscellaneous.
